x-fal-metadata.about:
Generate videos using Google's Veo 3.1 model.

For best results, prompts should be descriptive and clear. Include:
- Subject: What you want in the video (object, person, animal, scenery)
- Context: The background/setting
- Action: What the subject is doing
- Style: Film style keywords (horror, noir, cartoon etc.)
- Camera motion (optional): aerial view, tracking shot etc.
- Composition (optional): wide shot, close-up etc.
- Ambiance (optional): Color and lighting details

More details are available in our [prompting guide](https://blog.fal.ai/mastering-video-generation-with-veo-2-a-comprehensive-guide/).

The model supports:
- 720p or 1080p resolution videos
- 5-8 second duration at 24 FPS
- Both 16:9 (landscape) and 9:16 (portrait) aspect ratios

Safety filters prevent generation of inappropriate content.

x-fal-metadata.about:
Generate videos using Google's Veo 3 model.

For best results, prompts should be descriptive and clear. Include:
- Subject: What you want in the video (object, person, animal, scenery)
- Context: The background/setting
- Action: What the subject is doing
- Style: Film style keywords (horror, noir, cartoon etc.)
- Camera motion (optional): aerial view, tracking shot etc.
- Composition (optional): wide shot, close-up etc.
- Ambiance (optional): Color and lighting details

More details are available in our [prompting guide](https://blog.fal.ai/mastering-video-generation-with-veo-2-a-comprehensive-guide/).

The model supports:
- 720p or 1080p resolution videos
- 5-8 second duration at 24 FPS
- Both 16:9 (landscape) and 9:16 (portrait) aspect ratios

Safety filters prevent generation of inappropriate content.

audio_url.description:
URL of the audio to use as the background music. Must be publicly accessible.
Limit handling: If the audio duration exceeds the duration value (5 or 10 seconds),
the audio is truncated to the first 5 or 10 seconds, and the rest is discarded. If
the audio is shorter than the video, the remaining part of the video will be silent.
For example, if the audio is 3 seconds long and the video duration is 5 seconds, the
first 3 seconds of the output video will have sound, and the last 2 seconds will be silent.
- Format: WAV, MP3.

- File size: Up to 15 MB.

x-fal-metadata.about:
LTX Video - Text to Video generation

See examples for more inspiration.

### Instructions
When writing prompts, focus on detailed, chronological descriptions of actions and scenes. Include specific movements,
appearances, camera angles, and environmental details - all in a single flowing paragraph. Start directly with the action,
and keep descriptions literal and precise. Think like a cinematographer describing a shot list. Keep within 200 words. For
best results, build your prompts using this structure:
* Start with main action in a single sentence
* Add specific details about movements and gestures
* Describe character/object appearances precisely
* Include background and environment details
* Specify camera angles and movements
* Describe lighting and colors
* Note any changes or sudden events

### Parameter Guide
* Guidance Scale: Higher values (5-7) for accurate prompt following, lower values (3-5) for more creative freedom
* Inference Steps: More steps (40+) for quality, fewer steps (20-30) for speed

examples: A man stands waist-deep in a crystal-clear mountain pool, his back turned to a massive, thundering waterfall that cascades down jagged cliffs behind him. He wears a dark blue swimming shorts and his muscular back glistens with water droplets. The camera moves in a dynamic circular motion around him, starting from his right side and sweeping left, maintaining a slightly low angle that emphasizes the towering height of the waterfall. As the camera moves, the man slowly turns his head to follow its movement, his expression one of awe as he gazes up at the natural wonder. The waterfall creates a misty atmosphere, with sunlight filtering through the spray to create rainbow refractions. The water churns and ripples around him, reflecting the dramatic landscape. The handheld camera movement adds a subtle shake that enhances the raw, untamed energy of the scene. The lighting is natural and bright, with the sun positioned behind the waterfall, creating a backlit effect that silhouettes the falling water and illuminates the mist.

- Better preservation of artistic style and aesthetics

info.description: The OpenAPI schema for the fal-ai/veo2 queue.

x-fal-metadata.about:
Generate videos using Google's [Veo 2 text-to-video model](https://blog.google/technology/google-labs/video-image-generation-update-december-2024/).

For best results, prompts should be descriptive and clear. Include:
- Subject: What you want in the video (object, person, animal, scenery)
- Context: The background/setting
- Action: What the subject is doing
- Style: Film style keywords (horror, noir, cartoon etc.)
- Camera motion (optional): aerial view, tracking shot etc.
- Composition (optional): wide shot, close-up etc.
- Ambiance (optional): Color and lighting details

More details are available in our [prompting guide](https://blog.fal.ai/mastering-video-generation-with-veo-2-a-comprehensive-guide/).

The model supports:
- 720p resolution videos
- 5-8 second duration at 24 FPS
- Both 16:9 (landscape) and 9:16 (portrait) aspect ratios

Safety filters prevent generation of inappropriate content.

prompt.title: Prompt
examples: The camera floats gently through rows of pastel-painted wooden beehives, buzzing honeybees gliding in and out of frame. The motion settles on the refined farmer standing at the center, his pristine white beekeeping suit gleaming in the golden afternoon light. He lifts a jar of honey, tilting it slightly to catch the light. Behind him, tall sunflowers sway rhythmically in the breeze, their petals glowing in the warm sunlight. The camera tilts upward to reveal a retro farmhouse with mint-green shutters, its walls dappled with shadows from swaying trees. Shot with a 35mm lens on Kodak Portra 400 film, the golden light creates rich textures on the farmer's gloves, marmalade jar, and weathered wood of the beehives.

metadata.description: Create natural HeyGen Avatar V digital twin videos from text or audio, with lip-sync, optional backgrounds, captions, and MP4/WebM output.

info.title: Queue OpenAPI for fal-ai/heygen/avatar5/digital-twin
info.description: The OpenAPI schema for the fal-ai/heygen/avatar5/digital-twin queue.

x-fal-metadata.about:
Generate digital twin videos using HeyGen Avatar V.

Avatar V is HeyGen's newest avatar rendering engine. It uses
cross-reference-driven animation for more natural motion and
lip-sync than Avatar IV, especially for complex expressions and
longer scripts. Avatar V is only available through HeyGen's v3
API — this endpoint sets ``engine={"type": "avatar_v"}``
automatically.

Supports the v3 Avatar V feature set: ``fit``, ``remove_background``,
``background``, ``watermark``, ``caption``, and webm output.

Billed per second of output video ($0.1500/s).

background.description: Optional background to composite behind the avatar. Ignored when ``output_format='webm'`` (webm output is transparent).

resolution.title: Resolution
resolution.description: Output resolution preset.

aspect_ratio.title: Aspect Ratio
aspect_ratio.description: Aspect ratio of the output video. Supported values: '16:9', '9:16', '4:5', '5:4', '1:1', and 'auto'. 'auto' preserves the source aspect ratio when HeyGen can read it, falling back to '16:9' otherwise.

caption.title: Caption
caption.description: Generate a sidecar SRT caption file alongside the video.

voice.title: Voice
voice.description: Name of the text-to-speech voice to use for the avatar when ``audio_url`` is not provided.

remove_background.title: Remove Background
remove_background.description: Remove the avatar's background. Requires a matting-enabled avatar.

watermark.description: Optional watermark image to overlay on the output video.

audio_url.title: Audio Url
audio_url.description: HTTP(S) URL of an audio file for the avatar to lip-sync to. When provided, the avatar uses this audio instead of text-to-speech and ``prompt``/``voice`` are ignored.

fit.title: Fit
fit.description: How the avatar fits within the output frame. 'contain' keeps the full avatar in view (may letterbox); 'cover' fills the frame (may crop).

examples: Gala Casual Sofa with iPad Front
Gala Casual Sofa with iPad Side 2
Gala Casual Sofa with iPad Side
Raul Casual Sofa no iPad Front
Raul Casual Sofa no iPad Side
Raul Casual Sofa with iPad Front
Raul Casual Sofa with iPad Side
avatar.title: Avatar
avatar.description: Name of the Avatar V-eligible avatar to use.

examples: Hello and welcome to this demonstration of HeyGen Avatar V.
prompt.title: Prompt
prompt.description: Text the avatar will speak. Required when ``audio_url`` is not provided.

output_format.title: Output Format
output_format.description: Output container format. 'webm' produces a transparent video (automatically removes the background) and ignores the ``background`` field.

HeygenAvatar5DigitalTwinInput.title: AvatarVRequest
HeygenAvatar5DigitalTwinInput.description:
Request for HeyGen Avatar V digital-twin video generation.

Avatar V is HeyGen's newest avatar rendering engine — it uses
cross-reference-driven animation for more natural motion and lip-sync
than Avatar IV, particularly for complex expressions and longer
scripts. It is only available through HeyGen's v3 API and requires
explicit opt-in via ``engine={"type": "avatar_v"}``, which this
endpoint sets automatically.

Pass either ``avatar`` (a name from the catalog returned by
``/v2/avatars``) or ``avatar_id`` (a raw HeyGen avatar ID). The
avatar must be Avatar V-eligible — HeyGen returns a 400 error for
ineligible avatars.

video.description: The generated Avatar V video file.

caption_file.description: Generated caption file (SRT) when ``caption=True`` and HeyGen returns one.

AvatarVBackground.title: AvatarVBackground
AvatarVBackground.description:
Background configuration for Avatar V videos.

Either a solid color (``type="color"``) or an image URL
(``type="image"``). Background is ignored when
``output_format="webm"`` because webm output forces a transparent
(background-removed) result.

frames_per_second.description: Frames per second of the generated video. Must be between 4 to 60. When using interpolation and `adjust_fps_for_interpolation` is set to true (default true,) the final FPS will be multiplied by the number of interpolated frames plus one. For example, if the generated frames per second is 16 and the number of interpolated frames is 1, the final frames per second will be 32. If `adjust_fps_for_interpolation` is set to false, this value will be used as-is.

acceleration.description: Acceleration level to use. The more acceleration, the faster the generation, but with lower quality. The recommended value is 'regular'.

video_write_mode.description: The write mode of the output video. Faster write mode means faster results but larger file size, balanced write mode is a good compromise between speed and quality, and small write mode is the slowest but produces the smallest file size.

prompt.description: Text prompt for video generation. Camera movement instructions can be added using square brackets (e.g. [Pan left] or [Zoom in]). You can use up to 3 combined movements per prompt. Supported movements: Truck left/right, Pan left/right, Push in/Pull out, Pedestal up/down, Tilt up/down, Zoom in/out, Shake, Tracking shot, Static shot. For example: [Truck left, Pan right, Zoom in]. For a more detailed guide, refer https://sixth-switch-2ac.notion.site/T2V-01-Director-Model-Tutorial-with-camera-movement-1886c20a98eb80f395b8e05291ad8645
examples: [Push in]Close up of a tense woman looks to the left, startled by a sound, in a darkened kitchen, Pots and pans hang ominously, the window in the kitchen is open and the wind softly blows the pans and creates an ominous mood. [Shake]the woman's shock turns to fear. Black-and-white film noir shot dimly lit, 1950s-style, with dramatic, high-contrast shadows. The overall atmosphere is reminiscent of Alfred Hitchcock's suspenseful storytelling, evoking a looming sense of dread with stark chiaroscuro lighting and a slight film-grain texture.

frames_per_second.description: Frames per second of the generated video. Must be between 4 to 60. When using interpolation and `adjust_fps_for_interpolation` is set to true (default true,) the final FPS will be multiplied by the number of interpolated frames plus one. For example, if the generated frames per second is 16 and the number of interpolated frames is 1, the final frames per second will be 32. If `adjust_fps_for_interpolation` is set to false, this value will be used as-is.

video_output_type.description: The output type of the generated video.

num_frames.title: Number of Frames
num_frames.description: The number of frames to generate.

camera_lora.title: Camera LoRA
camera_lora.description: The camera LoRA to use. This allows you to control the camera movement of the generated video more accurately than just prompting the model to move the camera.

distill_lora_second_pass_scale.title: Distill Lora Second Pass Scale
distill_lora_second_pass_scale.description: The scale of the distill LoRA to use for the second and subsequent passes.

seed.title: Seed
